glossary.note: proposed_terminology agrégé A1; common+domain_tm à charger depuis 02_glossaires/ au runtime

concept_swaps.source_term: mettre les mains dans la configuration
concept_swaps.decision_note: A3 correctly adapted the French idiom into natural US English. No further cultural swap is needed.
A3 correctly rendered the instructional idiom as the standard US English phrase 'step by step.' No further adaptation is needed.
A3 resolved the French/European work-status term appropriately as the culturally portable US English term "freelancer" without implying a specific French legal status such as auto-entrepreneur.
A3 used "Instructor," which matches OpenClassrooms glossary guidance and avoids incorrectly implying an academic university role with "professor."

The A1 flag was not resolved: the A3 draft still points to the French banner asset. A4 cannot generate or infer a localized replacement asset URL without breaking media references, so the asset is conserved and escalated for human media replacement.

concept_swaps.source_term: Comparaison des critères techniques IKEv1 et IKEv2
concept_swaps.a3_draft_value: Image asset: https://user.oc-static.com/upload/2026/01/09/17679696756745_image5.png; caption translated as: Comparison of IKEv1 and IKEv2 technical criteria
concept_swaps.final_output: Image asset unchanged; caption and alt text are in English.
concept_swaps.decision_note: A3 translated the surrounding alt text and caption, but the underlying image/table asset remains unchanged and may still contain French text. Because A4 cannot recreate image content as editable HTML or swap to an unknown English asset, this is conserved and escalated as a critical media localization blocker.

concept_swaps.source_term: Comparaison des critères fonctionnels IKEv1 et IKEv2
concept_swaps.a3_draft_value: Image asset: https://user.oc-static.com/upload/2026/01/09/17679696868077_image6.png; caption translated as: Comparison of IKEv1 and IKEv2 functional criteria
concept_swaps.final_output: Image asset unchanged; caption and alt text are in English.
concept_swaps.decision_note: A3 translated the surrounding alt text and caption, but the underlying image/table asset remains unchanged and may still contain French text. Because A4 cannot recreate image content as editable HTML or swap to an unknown English asset, this is conserved and escalated as a critical media localization blocker.
The forbidden French recap heading is not present in A3. A3 correctly applied the common glossary requirement for an h4 recap heading: “Let's Recap!”

concept_swaps.source_term: Vous voici arrivé à l’instant de vérité
concept_swaps.decision_note: A3 correctly replaced the French idiomatic expression with a natural en-US instructional phrase. No further cultural adaptation needed.

The banner asset still uses an FR-labeled filename. A4 cannot create or verify media assets automatically without risking broken links, so the content is retained and escalated as a CRITICAL human media-localization blocker.

concept_swaps.a3_draft_value: Existing image assets retained; figure alt text remains in French for the Packet Tracer/router interface screenshot.
concept_swaps.final_output: Existing image assets retained; figure alt text remains in French for the Packet Tracer/router interface screenshot.
concept_swaps.decision_note: A3 did not replace or verify the screenshots against the English Cisco Packet Tracer UI. A4 cannot inspect or regenerate media assets, so this remains unchanged and is escalated for human review.
The forbidden French heading was correctly replaced by the required OpenClassrooms en-US heading. No blocker remains for this term.

Create an en-US banner version; current filename indicates a French-localized asset.
